spec_match.rationale: Touches core terms from the chromestatus summary but skips privacy-preserving, migrate-to-ua-ch — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (input) — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb hints at one angle but the concept page foregrounds different terminology — coverage is present but framing drifts.

edge_cases.rationale: Only the happy path is shown — no error handling, feature detection, or boundary inputs are surfaced.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the required canonical ChromeStatus self-heal link https://chromestatus.com/feature/6224706757853184.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on entropy-budget
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Reduced User-Agent strings by default introduces privacy-preserving, migrate-to-ua-ch but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on entropy-budget
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Reconcile the feature-index blurb with entropy-budget's framing
openQuestions.detail: The blurb on the feature index sets up one angle but the concept page foregrounds different terminology. Either update the blurb to match what the page actually shows, or surface the blurb's framing in the page's H1/lede so a visitor lands on a coherent story.

summary: entropy-budget for Reduced User-Agent strings by default has structural gaps: the rubric flags multiple fails (see open questions). Needs a pass to convert it from a placeholder into a real concept demo. ChromeStatus reference link resolved.
